channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trump executive order sanctions international court over Israel war crimes case
item.description: President Trump on Thursday issued an executive order sanctioning the International Criminal Court (ICC) over its pursuit of war crimes cases against Israel’s leader and its former defense minister.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Egypt lobbies against Trump plan to empty Gaza of Palestinians as Israel makes preparations
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel says it has begun preparations for the departure of Palestinians from Gaza despite international rejection of U.S. President Donald Trump’s plan to empty the territory of its population.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US targets network accused of facilitating Iranian oil shipments to China with sanctions
item.description: The U.S. Department of the Treasury on Thursday announced new sanctions on an international network of individuals and companies accused of facilitating Iranian oil shipments to China.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: The sea was once a blessing for the Pakistani city of Gwadar. But it's become a curse
item.description: GWADAR, Pakistan (AP) — There was a time when few people in the coastal Pakistani city of Gwadar understood what climate change was. After a decade of extreme weather, many more do. Rain battered Gwadar for almost 30 consecutive hours last February.
item.dc:creator: RIAZAT BUTT, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Swedish police say mass shooter was connected to school where he opened fire
item.description: OREBRO, Sweden (AP) — The shooter who earlier this week killed 10 people in Sweden’s worst mass shooting was connected to the adult education center where he opened fire with at least one rifle-like weapon, law enforcement officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ALEKSANDAR FURTULA, SERGEI GRITS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Future looks dire for UN Palestinian refugee agency, says UNRWA chief
item.description: The head of the U_N_ agency for Palestinian refugees, Philippe Lazzarini, says an Israeli ban on his institution has not forced it to halt its services so far but that in the long run, the agency faces an “existential threat.”.
item.dc:creator: Abby Sewell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Panama's president: No deal to allow US warships to use canal for free
item.description: Panamanian President José Raúl Mulino on Thursday rejected U.S. claims that American warships and boats can transit the Panama Canal without paying a standard fee.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: From fighting disease to protecting the Amazon rainforest, USAID has big impact across the globe
item.description: The Trump administration’s decision to close the U.S. Agency for International Development has drawn widespread criticism from congressional Democrats and raised questions and concern about the influence billionaire ally Elon Musk wields over the federal government.
item.dc:creator: KIMBERLEE KRUESI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump and 'no one else' can end the Ukraine-Russia war, US ally says
item.description: Hungarian Foreign Minister Peter Szijjártó told Fox News Digital that President Donald Trump has the necessary credibility with both Ukraine and Russia to end the war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bill Gates hopeful Trump will see 'moral and strategic value' in AIDS relief program
item.description: Billionaire philanthropist Bill Gates in an interview Wednesday said he hopes President Trump sees the value in preserving the President's Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief (PEPFAR) program, which has saved more than 25 million lives since it launched in 2003.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: US imposes sanctions on network that helps ship Iranian oil to China
item.description: The U.S. on Thursday imposed sanctions on a network of more than a dozen people and firms that are accused of facilitating the shipment of millions of barrels of Iranian oil to China.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Liz Cheney knocks Musk on citizenship in defense of USAID work
item.description: Former Rep. Liz Cheney (R-Wyo.) slammed tech billionaire Elon Musk over his citizenship while attempting to defend the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) amid the Trump administration's perceived efforts to gut the agency.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Axelrod: 'Trump will be well satisfied to have this fight' on USAID
item.description: Democratic strategist David Axelrod weighed in on the Trump administration's efforts to dismantle the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID), saying President Trump “will be well satisfied to have this fight.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Zelenskyy to lead Ukraine’s delegation at Munich Security Conference which JD Vance will attend
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy will lead Ukraine’s delegation at the Munich Security Conference next week, where U.S. Vice President J.
item.dc:creator: Illia Novikov, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Opposition lawmakers protest alleged mistreatment of Indian deportees by US
item.description: India’s Parliament was disrupted as opposition lawmakers protested alleged mistreatment of 104 Indian citizens deported by the United States.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Two years on, survivors of Turkish earthquake still struggle with loss and hardship
item.description: Two years on from a devastating earthquake that shattered Turkey's southern region, nearly half a million people remain in temporary “container cities.”.
item.dc:creator: Suzan Fraser, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mexico deploys the first of 10,000 National Guard troops to US border after Trump's tariff threat
item.description: CIUDAD JUÁREZ, Mexico (AP) — A line of Mexican National Guard and Army trucks rumbled along the border separating Ciudad Juárez and El Paso, Texas, on Wednesday, among the first of 10,000 troops Mexico has sent to its northern frontier following tariff threats by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Argentina's president bans gender-affirming care for people under age 18
item.description: BUENOS AIRES (AP) — Argentina’s president has signed a decree banning gender-affirming care for people under age 18, a presidential spokesperson said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sweden's worst mass shooting leaves at least 11 dead, including the gunman
item.description: OREBRO, Sweden (AP) — Sweden's worst mass shooting left at least 11 people dead, including the gunman, and at least five seriously wounded at an adult education center west of Stockholm.
item.dc:creator: ALEKSANDAR FURTULA, SERGEI GRITS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN chief sounds the alarm amid fears over possible DOGE-inspired cuts after Trump's order
item.description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres sends a letter to U.N. staff as President Donald Trump makes sweeping changes to U.S. foreign aid and funding.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 10 killed at adult education center in what officials say is Sweden's worst mass shooting
item.description: At least 10 people, including the gunman, were killed Tuesday at an adult education center in what Sweden’s prime minister called the country’s worst mass shooting. But a final death toll, a conclusive number of wounded and a motive hadn’t yet been determined hours later.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Greek island Santorini evacuated after earthquakes shake tourist destination
item.description: Hundreds of earthquakes that have rattled the Greek islands since last week have prompted the evacuation of thousands from the tourist hotspot of Santorini.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: PM declares 'worst mass shooting in Swedish history' after gunman kills 10 on school campus
item.description: Swedish police are responding to a school shooting in Orebro, Sweden, on Tuesday after reports said at least 10 people were killed
item.dc:creator: Fox News
